evals.prompt: Run a performance audit on src/lib/dedupe.ts. Look at the algorithms and concurrency and tell me the hot spots.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches two performance problems in src/lib/dedupe.ts: (D5) findDuplicates is O(n^2) — a nested for-loop compares every pair of items, quadratic in the list size; and (D8) enrich awaits fetchOne sequentially inside a for-loop, so N ids incur N serial round-trips instead of running concurrently. Each is reported at its file:line with an impact model / severity (the quadratic scan and serial I/O both degrade with scale). The audit writes its report to zuvo/audits/ and modifies no source file.
assertions: The audit records an O(n^2) / quadratic-complexity finding against findDuplicates in src/lib/dedupe.ts — the nested for-loop comparing every pair of items — at its file:line.
The audit records a sequential-await / serial-I/O finding against enrich in src/lib/dedupe.ts — fetchOne is awaited one id at a time inside the loop with no concurrency — at its file:line.
The audit writes a performance-audit report to zuvo/audits/ recording the quadratic and sequential-await findings with a severity/impact model, and it modifies no source file.

evals.prompt: Run a performance audit on src/lib/dedupe.ts and tell me if the algorithms and concurrency are OK.
evals.expected_output: The audit shows discipline, not loop-phobia: it does NOT flag an O(n^2) hot spot (findDuplicates is a Set-based O(n) single pass — each id is an O(1) has/add, not a nested pairwise scan), and it does NOT flag a sequential-await problem (enrich uses Promise.all over ids.map, firing the fetches concurrently). It records a healthy algorithms/concurrency result and writes its report to zuvo/audits/, modifying no source file — no fabricated quadratic or serial-I/O finding.
assertions: The audit records no O(n^2) / quadratic-complexity finding against findDuplicates in src/lib/dedupe.ts — the Set-based single-pass dedupe is recognized as O(n), not a nested-loop hot spot.
The audit records no sequential-await / serial-I/O finding against enrich in src/lib/dedupe.ts — Promise.all over ids.map fires the fetches concurrently, recognized as correct.
The audit writes a performance-audit report to zuvo/audits/ recording a healthy algorithms/concurrency result, and it modifies no source file.
